steps.title: Write the quadratic formula
steps.content:
Put the equation in standard form.
$$6x^2-11x+c-4=0$$
The quadratic formula gives
$$x=\frac{11\pm\sqrt{(-11)^2-4(6)(c-4)}}{12}$$
$$x=\frac{11\pm\sqrt{217-24c}}{12}$$

steps.title: Match the radical
steps.content:
The given solution has $\sqrt{601}$ in the numerator, so set the discriminant equal to $601$.
$$217-24c=601$$
$$-24c=384$$
$$c=-16$$
The answer is -16.
